name: Slice 1: Hook script — state files + terminal tab titles
decisions_by_ai: Added version: 1 field to state files as recommended in plan open question #3 for future-proofing
Used printf with \033 for OSC 0 escape sequences (portable across shells)
Sanitized session_id for filename safety by replacing non-alphanumeric chars (except dash/underscore) with underscores — prevents path traversal attacks
Server auto-launch logic (A1.7, A1.8) deferred to Slice 3 per plan — not included in this slice
diversions_from_plan: Markdown stripping uses sed range deletion for code fences (```...```) instead of single-line pattern, since multiline code blocks span multiple lines
The strip_markdown function uses printf instead of echo to handle escape sequences correctly from jq output
aha_moments: sed uses \( \) for capture groups in basic mode, not ( ) — the initial implementation had invalid backreferences for link/image patterns
jq -r properly converts JSON \n to real newlines, but sed line-anchored patterns (^#) need the input to have actual newlines to work — printf '%s' preserves them correctly
test_instructions: 1. Run: echo '{"session_id":"test-123","cwd":"/tmp/test"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh init
2. Check ~/.claude/tab-state/test-123.json exists with status 'starting'
3. Run: echo '{"session_id":"test-123"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh working
4. Verify status changed to 'working' in the JSON file
5. Run: echo '{"session_id":"test-123","last_assistant_message":"## Done\n**All** tests pass"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh stop
6. Verify status is 'done' and last_message_preview has markdown stripped
7. Run: echo '{"session_id":"test-123"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh cleanup
8. Verify the state file is deleted
9. To test with real Claude Code, add hook config to ~/.claude/settings.json per the PRD hook configuration section

name: Slice 2: Express server — JSON API + file watching + SSE
decisions_by_ai: Used native fs.watch instead of chokidar for file watching — chokidar failed to detect events in Docker/container environments while fs.watch works reliably. This also eliminates a dependency.
PID liveness uses a cache (Map) that is refreshed every 60s, with on-demand checks for uncached PIDs on API requests
SSE broadcasts a simple 'reload' signal rather than sending diff data — client re-fetches /api/sessions for full state, keeping the protocol simple as specified in PRD
PID file is cleaned up on process exit via 'exit' event handler with SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers
Invalid JSON state files are silently skipped with a console warning rather than crashing the server
diversions_from_plan: Replaced chokidar with native Node.js fs.watch — the plan specified chokidar but it failed in Docker environments. fs.watch works with zero dependencies and the macOS fsevents backend is equally performant.
The chokidar dependency was removed from package.json entirely
aha_moments: chokidar (v3) completely failed to emit events in the Docker/container test environment even with usePolling enabled, while native fs.watch worked perfectly — a reminder that native APIs can be more reliable than popular libraries in constrained environments
fs.watch provides the filename parameter on Linux/macOS which is sufficient for filtering .json files — no need for the more complex glob patterns chokidar offers
test_instructions: 1. Start server: cd server && node server.js
2. In another terminal: curl http://127.0.0.1:3847/api/health — should return {"ok":true}
3. curl http://127.0.0.1:3847/api/sessions — should return [] (empty array)
4. Create a session: echo '{"session_id":"test-1","cwd":"/tmp/test"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh init
5. curl http://127.0.0.1:3847/api/sessions — should return array with one session including alive: true/false
6. Test SSE: curl -N http://127.0.0.1:3847/api/events (keep running), then in another terminal create/modify/delete state files — SSE events should appear
7. Test port conflict: start a second server on same port — should exit with clear error
8. Test custom port: PORT=4444 node server.js — should bind to port 4444
9. Test pruning: create a state file with old mtime (touch -t 202603230000 file), restart server, verify it's deleted
10. Test invalid JSON: echo 'not json' > ~/.claude/tab-state/bad.json, then curl /api/sessions — bad file should be skipped

name: Slice 3: Hook → server auto-launch
decisions_by_ai: The auto_launch_server function is defined as a bash function and only called from the init event handler, keeping other events fast (no curl overhead)
Dead PID recovery: if PID file exists but the process is dead, the stale PID file is removed before starting a new server
Server script location uses ~/.claude/dashboard/server.js matching the PRD file structure — requires server files to be copied there (done by install.sh in Slice 6)
The function returns early (return 1) with a stderr warning if the server script doesn't exist, rather than failing the entire hook — the state file is still written successfully
diversions_from_plan: The plan mentions writing PID to server.pid from the hook, but the server already writes its own PID file on startup (implemented in Slice 2). The hook relies on the server's PID file for race condition detection instead of writing it separately.
aha_moments: The server already handles PID file creation in server.js (written in Slice 2), so the hook only needs to READ the PID file for race condition guarding, not write it. This avoids a race where the hook writes a PID but the server hasn't started listening yet.
test_instructions: 1. Ensure server is NOT running: kill any existing server process and remove PID file
2. Run: echo '{"session_id":"test-auto","cwd":"/tmp/test"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh init
3. Wait 2 seconds, then: curl http://127.0.0.1:3847/api/health — should return {"ok":true}
4. Note the PID: cat ~/.claude/dashboard/server.pid
5. Run a second init: echo '{"session_id":"test-auto-2","cwd":"/tmp/test2"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh init
6. Verify PID unchanged: cat ~/.claude/dashboard/server.pid — should match step 4
7. Kill the server, write a fake dead PID: echo 99999 > ~/.claude/dashboard/server.pid
8. Run init again — server should detect dead PID, clean up, and start a new server
9. Verify: curl http://127.0.0.1:3847/api/health returns {"ok":true}
10. Clean up: rm ~/.claude/tab-state/test-auto*.json

name: Slice 4: Browser dashboard — static cards (no SSE yet)
decisions_by_ai: Used Vite scaffolding with vue template, then stripped all boilerplate (HelloWorld, style.css, assets/) to start clean
Added vite.config.js proxy for /api -> localhost:3847 to support dev mode with hot reload (per plan open question #2)
Project groups sorted alphabetically by project name for consistent ordering across refreshes
Open and Copy ID buttons included in this slice (UI only) — the deep-link logic is trivial and there's no reason to defer the button markup to Slice 6
SPA fallback route added to server with regex /^(?!\/api).*/ to serve index.html for client-side routing
diversions_from_plan: Plan says deep-links/actions belong to Slice 6, but the Open and Copy ID buttons are included in the card component already since the UI markup is minimal and the functionality is self-contained (window.open + clipboard API)
No separate favicon — removed the scaffold's favicon reference to keep things simple
aha_moments: The SPA fallback route in Express must be placed AFTER express.static() but the regex negative lookahead /^(?!\/api).*/ correctly avoids intercepting API routes
test_instructions: 1. Start server: cd server && node server.js
2. Open http://127.0.0.1:3847 in a browser — should show 'No active sessions' empty state
3. Create mock sessions: write JSON files to ~/.claude/tab-state/ with different statuses and cwds
4. Refresh browser — cards should appear grouped by project name (last segment of cwd)
5. Verify card elements: status emoji, session name (truncated at 15 chars), relative time, preview text
6. Verify cards with alive:false are grayed out with 'Session ended unexpectedly' label
7. Verify left border colors match status (green=working, blue=done, amber=attention, red=error, gray=starting)
8. Toggle OS dark mode — verify theme switches (background, text, card colors)
9. Test static assets: verify JS and CSS files load (check browser network tab)
10. Clean up: remove test session files from ~/.claude/tab-state/

name: Slice 5: Live updates — SSE + connection status
decisions_by_ai: Used a 10-second timeout before transitioning from 'reconnecting' to 'disconnected' — gives EventSource's built-in auto-reconnect time to succeed before showing the worst-case indicator
On every SSE (re)connect, fresh data is fetched via fetchSessions() to ensure the dashboard is up-to-date after any period of disconnection
Added retry: 3000 to SSE initial response to configure EventSource's built-in 3-second reconnect interval as specified in the plan
ConnectionStatus component uses scoped CSS with the status class on the wrapper div for clean selector targeting
diversions_from_plan: No diversions — implementation matches the plan scope exactly
aha_moments: EventSource's onopen fires on reconnection too, not just initial connect — this is ideal for triggering a fresh fetchSessions() automatically after reconnect without needing separate logic
test_instructions: 1. Start server: cd server && node server.js
2. Open http://127.0.0.1:3847 in a browser — header should show '● Connected' with green dot

3. Create a session file: echo '{"session_id":"live-1","cwd":"/tmp/test","status":"working","name":"live-test","pid":1,"last_activity":"2026-03-25T10:00:00Z","created_at":"2026-03-25T10:00:00Z"}' > ~/.claude/tab-state/live-1.json
4. Card should appear on dashboard WITHOUT page refresh
5. Modify the file to change status to 'done' — card should update live
6. Delete the file: rm ~/.claude/tab-state/live-1.json — card should disappear
7. Stop the server (Ctrl+C) — indicator should change to '● Reconnecting…' (amber) then '● Disconnected' (red)
8. Restart the server — indicator should return to '● Connected' (green) and data should refresh

name: Slice 6: Deep-links + actions + install script
decisions_by_ai: Open and Copy ID buttons were already implemented in Slice 4's SessionCard.vue — no additional work needed for D1/D2 functionality
install.sh uses rm -rf on dist/ before copying to ensure clean overwrites on re-install
Hook script is chmod +x'd during install for direct execution
The install script prints both Claude hook config AND VS Code terminal tab settings needed for OSC 0 titles
diversions_from_plan: The plan listed deep-links and button actions as Slice 6 scope, but these were already implemented in Slice 4 (SessionCard.vue already had fully functional Open and Copy ID buttons). Slice 6 was therefore focused entirely on the install.sh script.
aha_moments: The deep-link and copy ID functionality was trivial enough that it was naturally included in Slice 4 when building the card component — the plan's deferral to Slice 6 was overly conservative
test_instructions: 1. Run: bash install.sh
2. Verify ~/.claude/tab-state/ directory exists
3. Verify ~/.claude/hooks/tab-state.sh exists and is executable
4. Verify ~/.claude/dashboard/dist/index.html exists
5. Verify ~/.claude/dashboard/server.js exists
6. Verify ~/.claude/dashboard/node_modules/express/ exists
7. Run install.sh a second time — should complete without errors (idempotency)
8. Verify the script prints hook configuration JSON and VS Code settings (does NOT overwrite settings.json)
9. Start server: node ~/.claude/dashboard/server.js
10. Open http://127.0.0.1:3847 — dashboard should load
11. Create test session files and verify cards appear with Open/Copy ID buttons
12. Click 'Open' button — should trigger vscode:// URI
13. Click 'Copy ID' — should copy session ID and show 'Copied!' feedback
